channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Wave of Israeli strikes hit Yemen's main airport and key infrastructure
item.description: Houthi rebels in Yemen say Israeli airstrikes targeted the rebel-held capital of Sanaa and the port city of Hodeida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: More than 10,000 migrants died this year trying to reach Spain by sea, aid group says
item.description: MADRID (AP) — More than 10,000 migrants died while trying to reach Spain by sea this year, a report released by a Spanish migration rights group said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia arrests 4 suspects accused of plotting to kill top military officers on Ukraine's orders
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top security agency said Thursday that it has arrested several suspects accused of involvement in an alleged Ukrainian plot to assassinate senior military officers, an announcement that follows the killing of a top Russian general last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Azerbaijan observes day of mourning for air crash victims as speculation mounts about its cause
item.description: Azerbaijan is observing a nationwide day of mourning for the victims of the air crash that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured as speculation mounted about a possible cause of the crash that remained unknown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Incoming Trump admin, Congress showdown looms with South Africa over support for Russia, US foes
item.description: Senators and analysts tell Fox News Digital the incoming Trump administration could shut down South Africa’s AGOA trade deals if Pretoria doesn’t review its relations with U.S. foes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean opposition submits motion to impeach the country's acting president as strife deepens
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s main opposition party submitted a motion on Thursday to impeach the country’s acting leader over his reluctance to fill three Constitutional Court vacancies ahead of the court’s review of rebellion charges against impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol stemming fro
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A survivor of the worst gang attack on Haitian journalists says colleagues were cut down by bullets
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A survivor of the worst gang attack on Haitian journalists in recent memory described Wednesday seeing colleagues cut down by bullets and reporters with head and chest wounds going an hour or more without help.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE LUXAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner crashes in Kazakhstan, killing 38 with 29 survivors, officials say
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, killing 38 people and leaving 29 survivors, a Kazakh official said.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prayers and tears mark 20 years since the Indian Ocean tsunami that killed some 230,000 people
item.description: People are gathering in prayer and visiting mass graves in Indonesia’s Aceh province to mark 20 years since the massive Indian Ocean tsunami, one of modern history’s worst natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Reza Saifullah And Edna Tarigan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A baby froze to death overnight in Gaza as Israel and Hamas trade accusations of ceasefire delays
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A baby girl froze to death overnight in Gaza, while Israel and Hamas accused each other of complicating ceasefire efforts that could wind down the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, TIA GOLDENBERG and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia being blamed for Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed hundreds of miles off course, killing dozens
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky condemns Putin for 'inhumane' Christmas Day attack
item.description: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelensky condemned Russia’s President Vladimir Putin for greenlighting an “inhumane” Christmas Day attack, including missiles and drones, that damaged Ukraine’s energy infrastructure and left thousands with power outages.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — In a dim one-room apartment in one of Istanbul’s poorest neighborhoods, 11-year-old Atakan Sahin curls up on a threadbare sofa with his siblings to watch TV while their mother stirs a pot of pasta. The simple meal is all the family of six can look forward to most evenings.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Haiti gang attack on the reopening of a main hospital kills 2 reporters and a police officer
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Two reporters were killed and several were wounded Tuesday in a gang attack in Haiti on the reopening of Port-au-Prince’s biggest public hospital, the country's online media association said. A police officer was also killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After 20 years, the post-tsunami generation stays vigilant for future disasters
item.description: LHOKNGA, Indonesia (AP) — Qurrata Ayuni, a 28-year-old survivor of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated her hometown two decades ago, has transformed her resilience into purpose.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Toyota to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration is getting $1 million from Toyota Motor North America. A spokesperson for Toyota confirmed the inauguration donation to The Hill on Tuesday. Toyota joins Ford and a number of other companies in pledging significant donations to Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: France has a new government, again. Politics and crushing debt complicate next steps
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s president and prime minister managed to form a new government just in time for the holidays. Now comes the hard part.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's threats to retake the Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Trump is sparking a new feud with Panama over an old topic: the vital maritime canal connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Iran recruiting children to attack Israeli targets across Europe amid setbacks: report
item.description: Iran has been hiring young people in Europe to attack Israeli and Jewish targets in countries across the continent, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Projectile from Yemen strikes near Tel Aviv, injuring more than a dozen: officials
item.description: A projectile launched into Israel from Yemen overnight struck Tel Aviv, leading to 16 people suffering mild injuries, according to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
